page: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 16 dedicated physical ARMv9 cores based on the Neoverse-V3 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration provides 16 threads with one thread per core and includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and features an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9g.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory and decompression capabilities. Its PassMark memory score ranks in the top tier, supported by a read-only small block cached memory bandwidth of 2282.43 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 78.36 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier performance levels at 36.42 GB/sec and 17.14 GB/sec, respectively. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput are also strong, with the latter reaching 54.3 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 376.71 tokens/sec on a small 135M model, alongside strong text generation performance of 30.2 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model.

The ARMv9 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high resource density and predictable performance, making this instance a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and strong database operation benchmarks, the m9g.4xlarge is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, text processing, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 64.0 GB of system memory, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. In benchmark testing, the m9g.4xlarge exhibits top-tier memory performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and static web serving throughput. It also delivers efficient text generation for small and medium language models. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and predictable dedicated core performance, this instance is highly suited for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: A balanced ARMv9 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Neoverse-V3 cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for predictable enterprise workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance
